reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. The auxiliary function f with the hypothesis f = λ g => g⁻¹ is merely a re-parameterization of "the map g ↦ g⁻¹". The left side of the iff, ∀ x y, f(x)·f(y) = f(x·y), unfolds to x⁻¹·y⁻¹ = (xy)⁻¹, which is exactly the group-homomorphism condition for this map (identity-preservation e⁻¹ = e is automatic, and the multiplicative condition is all that's needed). The right side, ∀ x y, x·y = y·x, is exactly "G is abelian". Both directions of the iff are mathematically equivalent to the informal claim. There are no extra hypotheses, no type/quantifier changes, and no narrowing of scope (G is an arbitrary group).
